organization.fullName: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

identificationModule.briefTitle: Study Evaluating 13-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Healthy Japanese Infants
identificationModule.officialTitle: A Phase 3, Open-Label Trial Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability, and Immunogenicity of a 13-valent Pneumococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Healthy Infants in Japan

leadSponsor.name: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this study is to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and immunogenicity of the 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) in infants of Japanese descent.

interventions.name: 13vPnC
interventions.description: "First dose during 2 to 6 months of age Second dose at least 28 days after the first dose Third dose at least 28 days after the second dose Forth dose during 12 to 15 months of age"

primaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Antibody Level ≥ 0.35 μg/mL in the 13vPnC Group After the 3-Dose Infant Series
primaryOutcomes.description: Percentages of participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥ 0.35 μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after 3-dose infant series (at 7 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration (GMC) in 13vPnC Group Relative After the 3-Dose Infant Series
secondaryOutcomes.description: GMC as measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one mone month after 3-dose infant series (at 7 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Achieving Antibody Level ≥ 0.35 μg/mL in the 13vPnC Group After the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.description: Percentages of participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥ 0.35 μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose (at 12 - 15 months of age)

secondaryOutcomes.measure: Geometric Mean Antibody Concentration (GMC) in 13vPnC Group Relative After the Toddler Dose
secondaryOutcomes.description: GMC as measured by enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) for 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (Serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: one month after the toddler dose (at 12-15 months of age)

otherOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Local Reactions
otherOutcomes.description: Local reactions were collected using an electronic diary. Tenderness was scaled as Any (tenderness present); Significant (present and interfered with limb movement). Swelling and redness were scaled as Any (swelling or redness present); Mild (0.5 centimeters \[cm\] to 2.0 cm); Moderate (2.5 to 7.0 cm); Severe (\>7.0 cm). Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.

otherOutcomes.measure: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Systemic Events
otherOutcomes.description: Systemic events (fever ≥ 37.5 degrees Celsius \[C\], fever ≥ 38 C but ≤ 39 C, fever \>39 C but ≤ 40 C, fever \> 40 C, decreased appetite, irritability, increased sleep, decreased sleep, hives, use of medication to treat symptoms, and use of medication to prevent symptoms) were reported using an electronic diary. Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Healthy 2 to 6 month-old infant
* Available for entire study period

Exclusion Criteria:

* Previous vaccination with licensed or investigational pneumococcal vaccine.
* Previous anaphylactic reaction to any vaccine or vaccine-related component.
* Contraindication to vaccination with a pneumococcal conjugate vaccine.
* Bleeding diathesis or condition associated with prolonged bleeding time that would contraindicate any type of injection.
* Known or suspected immune deficiency or suppression.

overallOfficials.name: Medical Monitor
overallOfficials.affiliation: Wyeth is now a wholly owned subsidiary of Pfizer

references.citation: Togashi T, Yamaji M, Thompson A, Giardina PC, Aizawa M, Patterson S, Gruber WC, Scott DA; 3003 Study Group. Immunogenicity and safety of a 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine in healthy infants in Japan. Pediatr Infect Dis J. 2013 Sep;32(9):984-9. doi: 10.1097/INF.0b013e318293007e.

participantFlowModule.preAssignmentDetails: Participants were enrolled into the study according to inclusion/exclusion criteria without a screening period.
participantFlowModule.recruitmentDetails: Participants were recruited in Japan from September 2007 to February 2008.

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) at approximately 2, 4, 6 months (infant series) and 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

groups.title: 13vPnC
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) at approximately 2, 4, 6 months (infant series) and 12-15 months of age (toddler dose).

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Achieving Antibody Level ≥ 0.35 μg/mL in the 13vPnC Group After the 3-Dose Infant Series
outcomeMeasures.description: Percentages of participants achieving World Health Organization (WHO) predefined antibody threshold ≥ 0.35 μg/mL along with the corresponding 95% CI for the 7 common pneumococcal serotypes (serotypes 4, 6B, 9V, 14, 18C, 19F, and 23F) and 6 additional pneumococcal serotypes specific to 13vPnC (serotypes 1, 3, 5, 6A, 7F, and 19A) are presented.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: Evaluable immunogenicity population had valid and determinate assay results and had no other major protocol violations.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: one month after 3-dose infant series (at 7 months of age)

groups.title: 13vPnC After Infant Series
groups.description: Participants received one single 0.5mL dose of 13-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (13vPnC) at approximately 2, 4, 6 months (infant series).

outcomeMeasures.title: Percentage of Participants Reporting Pre-Specified Local Reactions
outcomeMeasures.description: Local reactions were collected using an electronic diary. Tenderness was scaled as Any (tenderness present); Significant (present and interfered with limb movement). Swelling and redness were scaled as Any (swelling or redness present); Mild (0.5 centimeters \[cm\] to 2.0 cm); Moderate (2.5 to 7.0 cm); Severe (\>7.0 cm). Participants may be represented in more than 1 category.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The safety population included all participants who received at least 1 dose of vaccine, (n) = number of participants reporting yes for at least 1 day or no for all days.

certainAgreement.otherDetails: The PIs agreed to allow the sponsor 60 days to review and require changes to presentations or publications but only to protect confidential information and intellectual property, and for the sponsor to file a patent application, as applicable. The PIs also agreed for data to be presented first as a joint, multi-center publication.
